workspace.name: Nordic Supply Direct — AI Value Realization Demo

cards.text: Demand forecasts are frequently adjusted manually by planners and regional warehouse managers.
Inventory buffers are inconsistent across central warehouse, regional hubs, and high-volume product categories.
Nationwide distribution network with two central warehouses, eight regional hubs, and strong carrier partnerships.
High order volume creates rich historical demand, delivery, and customer service data.
Transport exceptions are triaged manually from emails, carrier portals, and phone calls.
Customer service spends significant time checking order status across ERP, WMS, TMS, and carrier sites.
Sales teams apply ad-hoc discounts to protect large accounts, causing margin leakage.
Product master data has inconsistent dimensions, substitutions, and packaging information.
Customer, order, inventory, and carrier data is fragmented across ERP, e-commerce, WMS, and TMS platforms.
AI recommendations could trigger wrong replenishment or delivery actions if not reviewed by planners.
Customer-specific contract pricing and delivery terms require strong access control and auditability.
Use AI to create a control-tower view for demand, inventory, transport exceptions, and service risk.
Offer premium delivery reliability insights to key B2B customers through the customer portal.

opportunities.text: AI demand forecasting for high-volume SKUs and regional hubs
Inventory balancing recommendations between central warehouse and regional hubs
Delivery exception triage assistant for late, damaged, or blocked shipments
Customer service order-status assistant with source links across ERP, WMS, TMS, and carrier portals
AI margin guidance for sales quotes and discount approvals
Data quality assistant for product dimensions, packaging, substitutions, and carrier constraints
AI logistics control tower combining demand, inventory, transport risk, and customer impact
Supplier lead-time early-warning model for critical product families

useCases.name: AI demand forecasting for high-volume SKUs and regional hubs
useCases.proposedSolution:
Role (Who): As a demand planner responsible for regional hub replenishment
Goal (What): I want AI-generated demand forecasts with drivers, confidence levels, and exception alerts
Benefit (Why): so that I can reduce stockouts and overstock while spending less time on manual forecast adjustments.
useCases.targetUsers: Demand planners, inventory managers, regional hub managers
useCases.expectedValue: Improved forecast accuracy, lower inventory buffers, fewer urgent replenishment actions
useCases.processImpact: Planning shifts from spreadsheet adjustment to exception review and approval
useCases.dataNeeds: Historical orders, promotions, seasonality, stock levels, supplier lead times, regional demand patterns
useCases.risks: Poor forecasts may create availability issues; planners need explanation and override capability
useCases.dependencies: ERP/WMS data pipeline, product hierarchy cleanup, planner review workflow
useCases.governance: Human approval required before replenishment parameter changes
useCases.integrationNeeds: ERP demand planning screen and replenishment workflow
useCases.compliance: Use internal operational data only; maintain forecast and override audit trail
useCases.kpis: Forecast accuracy, stockout rate, inventory turns, planner hours saved

useCases.name: Delivery exception triage assistant for late, damaged, or blocked shipments
useCases.proposedSolution:
Role (Who): As a transport coordinator monitoring nationwide deliveries
Goal (What): I want AI to classify delivery exceptions, identify customer impact, and suggest next best actions
Benefit (Why): so that I can resolve critical issues faster and prioritize shipments that affect important customers.
useCases.targetUsers: Transport coordinators, customer service agents, regional logistics managers
useCases.expectedValue: Faster exception resolution, fewer missed SLAs, better customer communication
useCases.processImpact: Manual inbox and portal checks become a prioritized exception queue with suggested actions
useCases.dataNeeds: TMS events, carrier updates, order priority, customer SLA, delivery history, contact templates
useCases.risks: Wrong prioritization or customer message could harm service experience
useCases.dependencies: Carrier event feed, TMS integration, customer SLA mapping
useCases.governance: AI suggests actions; coordinator approves customer-facing communication
useCases.modelType: Classification, retrieval, and generative message drafting
useCases.integrationNeeds: TMS exception queue and customer service case system
useCases.compliance: Customer data and contract SLA terms must remain in approved systems
useCases.kpis: Exception cycle time, SLA breach rate, proactive notifications, manual touches per exception

useCases.name: Customer service order-status assistant with source links across ERP, WMS, TMS, and carrier portals
useCases.proposedSolution:
Role (Who): As a customer service agent supporting B2B customers
Goal (What): I want a single AI assistant that summarizes order, stock, shipment, and carrier status with source links
Benefit (Why): so that I can answer customers faster and reduce handoffs to logistics and warehouse teams.
useCases.targetUsers: Customer service agents, key account support teams
useCases.expectedValue: Reduced average handling time, better first-contact resolution, improved customer satisfaction
useCases.processImpact: Agents move from searching several systems to validating an AI-generated answer with sources
useCases.dataNeeds: ERP order data, WMS pick/pack status, TMS shipment status, carrier tracking, customer notes
useCases.risks: Assistant may expose restricted customer or pricing information if access control is weak
useCases.dependencies: Identity/access model, system connectors, approved answer templates
useCases.governance: Source-linked answers; no autonomous customer commitment without human review
useCases.integrationNeeds: CRM/customer service console and links to operational systems
useCases.compliance: Respect customer-specific access rights and contract confidentiality
useCases.kpis: Average handling time, first-contact resolution, escalation rate, CSAT

roadmapItems.dependencies: Carrier event feed and TMS exception queue
Planning data pipeline and product hierarchy cleanup
